title: Memory Management & Pool Allocators
description: Understand custom memory structures, pool allocators, memory alignment, and cache performance.
learningObjectives: Differentiate general heap allocators (malloc) from specialized Pool Allocators
Identify padding and memory alignment requirements on 64-bit hardware
Prevent memory fragmentation issues by allocating memory in pre-defined segments

sections.title: Allocators and Alignment
sections.content:
General heap allocation (`malloc`/`new`) is slow because the OS search algorithm must locate open addresses. In high-performance game loops, we bypass this by implementing Custom Allocators:

1. **Linear (Arena) Allocator**: Allocates contiguous memory blocks, resets offset pointer to zero to clear all. Fast, zero fragmentation, but cannot delete single elements.
2. **Pool Allocator**: Divides a pre-allocated memory chunk into identical, fixed-size blocks. Deallocations return blocks to a free list. Ideal for constant instantiation/destruction of identical objects (bullets, particles).

sections.title: Structure Alignment and Padding
